channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Artistic Transformation of a Dutch Power Substation into a Turquoise Wall
item.description: In Beverwijk, Netherlands, Studio RAP has transformed a high-voltage power substation into an eye-catching ceramic wall titled 'Powerwall.' The project, part of their exploration of material and techn...

item.title: Harvard Researchers Develop Silicon Chip for DNA Synthesis
item.description: Harvard researchers have developed a silicon chip capable of synthesizing multiple DNA sequences simultaneously using water-based enzymatic reactions. This breakthrough, published in Nature Electronic...

item.title: Sudbury-area suspect faces arrest over assault incident
item.description: A warrant has been issued for the arrest of a suspect involved in an assault incident in the Sudbury area of Northern Ontario. The suspect, whose identity has not been disclosed, is alleged to have co...

item.title: Meta Launches Muse Image AI Tool with Controversial Data Usage Policies
item.description: Meta Platforms has launched Muse Image, an AI image generation tool that allows users to create images using text prompts or public Instagram photos. The feature is integrated into Meta AI and availab...

item.title: Government Interventions Aim to Reduce SA's Informal Settlements
item.description: Human settlements minister Thembi Simelane has acknowledged the significant challenges in eradicating South Africa's 4,200 informal settlements, emphasizing that the task is 'stacked against us'. She ...

item.title: Tanzania Private Sector Foundation Launches Strategic Partnership with International Chamber of Commerce
item.description: The Tanzania Private Sector Foundation (TPSF) has officially joined the International Chamber of Commerce (ICC) through a strategic partnership signed during the Tanzania-France Business Roundtable in...

item.title: Umzulu Phaqa on Building a Sustainable Music Career Through Consistency
item.description: Umzulu Phaqa, real name Sphiwe Moya, discusses her approach to building a sustainable music career, emphasizing consistency over chasing viral fame. After her song 'Mam’gobhozi' gained online traction...

item.title: South Africa Prepares for Potential Ebola Outbreak with Hospital Readiness and Screening Measures
item.description: The South African health department has confirmed that 24 public and 12 private hospitals are prepared to handle Ebola cases if they arrive in the country. This preparation includes the establishment ...

item.title: Kwa Binzaro Burial Practices Violate Giriama Traditions, Court Hears
item.description: A Kilifi area chief testified in court that victims of the Kwa Binzaro unlawful killings case were buried in ways that violated Giriama traditions. Chief Raymond Charo stated that bodies were placed i...

item.title: Supreme Court Allows Quota Benefits for Muslim Converts in Tamil Nadu
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that Muslim converts from Scheduled Castes (SC) communities in Tamil Nadu are eligible for reservation benefits under the state's affirmative action policies. This...

item.title: Nigeria Expresses Sympathy with China as Gansu Landslide Death Toll Rises to 21
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria has expressed its condolences to the government and people of the People's Republic of China following the deadly landslide that happened in Tanchang County, Gansu Pr...

item.title: Tensions at NATO Summit Under Trump Influence Raise Questions About Future Meetings
item.description: The recent NATO summit in Ankara highlighted significant tensions under President Trump's influence, with concerns about the future of annual leadership meetings. The event underscored Trump's disrupt...

item.title: Pakistan and Croatia Strengthen Trade and Connectivity Ties Through Diplomatic Engagement
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar highlighted Pakistan's strategic geographic location during discussions with Croatia's Foreign Minister Gordan Grlić Radic, emphasizing collaboration on port infrastruc...

item.title: Student Seized Army-Issued Handgun Attempting to Enter School
item.description: Philippine police successfully intercepted a 16-year-old student attempting to bring an Army-issued Glock 17 9mm handgun into his school in Tupi, South Cotabato. The incident occurred on July 7 when a...

item.title: Supreme Court Rules Disinheriting Loving Wife Raises Suspicion in Will Case
item.description: The Supreme Court (SC) has ruled that a will disinheriting a life-long loving wife and bequeathing property to distant relatives raises suspicion about its authenticity. The court emphasized that such...

item.title: British Columbia Review Board Approves Conditional Discharge for B.C. Child Killer with Oversight Measures
item.description: The British Columbia Review Board has approved a conditional discharge for Allan Schoenborn, the man found not criminally responsible for the 2008 murders of his three children in Merritt, B.C. The de...

item.title: South African Immigrants' Remittances: Key Insights from the Top 10 Countries
item.description: A report by FinMark Trust, supported by the South African Reserve Bank, reveals that nearly four million immigrants from neighboring countries send billions of rands monthly to their home countries. 8...

item.title: Review highlights flaws in disability benefit system, calls for overhaul
item.description: A review of the Personal Independence Payment (Pip) system in England and Wales has concluded that the disability benefit is 'not fit for purpose' and requires fundamental reform. Sir Stephen Timms, t...

item.title: Plateau State Passes Pension Bill for Former Lawmakers
item.description: The Plateau State House of Assembly has approved a pension bill for former lawmakers, including speakers, deputy speakers, and members. Sponsored by Eli Ankala (APC, Rukuba-Irigwe), the bill establish...

item.title: Semaglutide Misuse Leads to Surge in Poison Control Calls
item.description: The article discusses a significant rise in poison control calls involving semaglutide (Ozempic and Wegovy) following its FDA approval for weight management in 2021. Researchers linked the surge in ca...
